name: Binding Ruling Request to CBP
summary: Drafts a comprehensive and legally precise Binding Ruling Request to U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) pursuant to 19 CFR Part 177. This skill conducts thorough research on merchandise facts and customs law to establish requester standing and provide persuasive analysis for tariff classification, valuation, or other treatments. Use it when seeking binding guidance on customs compliance for prospective imports in international trade.
content:
# Enhanced Binding Ruling Request to U.S. Customs and Border Protection

You are tasked with drafting a comprehensive and legally precise Binding Ruling Request to U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) pursuant to 19 CFR Part 177. This formal regulatory document must demonstrate mastery of customs law, meticulous attention to procedural requirements, and persuasive legal analysis. A binding ruling provides definitive guidance on the tariff classification, valuation, country of origin marking, or other customs treatment of merchandise before importation, offering critical certainty for international trade compliance.

## Document Preparation and Research Foundation

Begin by conducting thorough research within any uploaded documents to identify all relevant factual information about the merchandise, prior importations, commercial arrangements, and business relationships. Search for technical specifications, manufacturing details, supply chain documentation, prior CBP correspondence, commercial invoices, and any previous customs determinations. Extract specific data points including material compositions with percentages, manufacturing processes, country of origin information, related party relationships, payment terms, and any prior ruling numbers or entry documentation. Verify all factual assertions against source documents and maintain precise attribution to ensure every statement in the ruling request can be substantiated with documentary evidence.

Supplement document-based research with targeted legal research to identify the controlling statutory framework, applicable regulations, relevant CBP rulings, and pertinent judicial decisions. For classification questions, locate analogous CBP headquarters rulings and New York rulings addressing similar merchandise, paying particular attention to factual parallels and the analytical reasoning employed. For valuation issues, research transaction value methodology under 19 USC 1401a and identify precedents addressing related party transactions, assists, royalties, or other valuation adjustments. Ensure all legal citations conform to Bluebook standards and verify the current validity of cited authorities.

## Requester Identification and Standing

Draft a comprehensive identification section that establishes the requester's legal standing to seek a binding ruling under 19 CFR 177.1(c). Present the complete legal name of the requesting entity as it appears in corporate formation documents, the principal place of business with full street address, direct telephone and email contact information, and the name and title of the authorized signatory. Articulate with precision the requester's relationship to the merchandise, whether as the actual importer of record, a prospective importer with demonstrable intent to import, the foreign manufacturer or exporter, or another party with a direct and substantial interest in the customs treatment determination.

When the request is submitted through legal counsel or a licensed customs broker, provide complete credentials including bar admission or customs broker license number, reference the executed power of attorney with its date and scope of authority, and confirm explicit authorization to act on behalf of the principal in customs matters. Address any potential standing issues proactively, particularly when the requester is not the ultimate importer, by explaining the commercial necessity for advance certainty and the requester's legitimate interest in the ruling determination.

## Comprehensive Merchandise Description

Develop an exhaustive technical description of the merchandise that enables CBP to render an informed determination without physical examination, though note that samples, photographs, or specimens will be submitted as supplementary evidence. The description must capture the essential character of the merchandise through multiple analytical lenses. Begin with the commercial designation and trade name, then systematically detail the material composition, providing specific percentages by weight or volume for all constituent materials and identifying each material by its technical or chemical name rather than generic terms.

Describe the manufacturing process in sufficient detail to understand how raw materials are transformed into the finished product, noting any critical steps that affect the merchandise's essential character or classification. Include precise dimensions, weight, capacity, or other quantitative specifications, and identify the primary function and intended commercial use with specificity regarding the end-user application and market sector. Reference applicable industry standards, technical certifications, or regulatory compliance requirements such as ASTM specifications, ISO standards, FDA approvals, or FCC certifications that characterize the merchandise within its commercial context.

If proposing a specific Harmonized Tariff Schedule classification, state the complete ten-digit HTS number and provide detailed analysis under the General Rules of Interpretation, explaining how GRI 1 through GRI 6 lead to the proposed classification. Reference the Harmonized System Explanatory Notes for the relevant heading and subheading, quoting language that supports the classification and addressing any exclusionary provisions. Distinguish the merchandise from products classified under alternative headings by identifying the specific characteristics that determine its essential character and tariff treatment.

## Factual Background and Transaction Structure

Present a complete factual narrative that establishes the commercial context for the ruling request and provides CBP with full transparency regarding all circumstances that might affect customs treatment. Describe the entire supply chain from raw material sourcing through manufacturing, intermediate processing, and final importation into the United States. Identify each entity involved in the commercial transaction including manufacturers, exporters, importers, distributors, and end-users, and explicitly address whether any related party relationships exist as defined under 19 CFR 152.102(g).

Detail the terms of sale using recognized Incoterms, specify the transaction currency and payment terms, and disclose any non-monetary consideration, assists provided to the foreign producer, royalty or license fee arrangements, or proceeds of subsequent resale that accrue to the seller. If the transaction involves related parties, provide the transfer pricing methodology, explain how the transaction value was determined, and address whether the relationship influenced the price under the test circumstances of 19 CFR 152.103(l).

When the merchandise or substantially similar merchandise has been previously imported, provide complete entry history including entry numbers, dates of entry, ports of entry, declared classifications and values, and the outcome of any CBP reviews, audits, or examinations. Reference any prior binding rulings, internal advice, or protest decisions that addressed the same or similar merchandise, and if circumstances have changed since those determinations, explain precisely what has changed in terms of product specifications, manufacturing processes, business relationships, or material facts.

## Precise Legal Questions Presented

Formulate each legal question with the precision necessary to elicit a definitive determination from CBP, ensuring that every question falls within the scope of the binding ruling program and CBP's administrative authority. Structure each question to address a single discrete issue, avoiding compound inquiries that conflate multiple legal determinations. For classification requests, frame the question to identify the specific HTS provision at issue: "Whether the subject merchandise is properly classifiable under subheading 8471.30.01, HTSUS, as portable automatic data processing machines weighing not more than 10 kg, or alternatively under subheading 8517.62.00, HTSUS, as machines for the reception, conversion and transmission or regeneration of voice, images or other data."

For valuation questions, identify the specific methodology and any adjustments at issue: "Whether the transaction value is acceptable as the basis for appraisement under 19 USC 1401a(b) when the buyer and seller are related parties, and if so, whether royalty payments for the use of trademarks must be added to the price actually paid or payable under 19 USC 1401a(b)(1)(D)." For country of origin determinations, reference the applicable legal framework: "Whether the country of origin for marking purposes under 19 CFR Part 102 is China, where the components are manufactured, or Mexico, where final assembly occurs and the product undergoes a change in tariff classification."

Ensure each question demonstrates understanding of CBP's jurisdictional limits by avoiding requests for advisory opinions on hypothetical future scenarios, interpretations of non-customs laws, or determinations regarding reasonable care, negligence, or fraud that fall outside the binding ruling program's scope.

## Persuasive Legal Analysis and Precedent Application

Construct a systematic legal analysis that applies controlling statutory provisions, implementing regulations, and CBP precedents to the specific facts of the merchandise and transaction. Begin with the foundational statutory authority, citing the relevant provisions of Title 19 United States Code and explaining how the statutory language governs the determination sought. For classification matters, methodically apply the General Rules of Interpretation in hierarchical order, demonstrating how GRI 1 requires classification according to the terms of the headings and any relative section or chapter notes, and proceeding through subsequent GRIs only when classification cannot be determined under preceding rules.

Incorporate the Harmonized System Explanatory Notes as persuasive authority, quoting relevant passages that describe the scope of headings or subheadings and explain how the merchandise's characteristics align with or diverge from the Explanatory Notes' guidance. Reference CBP's Informed Compliance Publications, What Every Member of the Trade Community Should Know About publications, and other official CBP guidance documents that provide interpretive context for classification, valuation, or marking determinations.

Identify and analyze analogous CBP rulings, prioritizing headquarters rulings for their precedential weight and citing New York rulings when they address factually similar merchandise. For each cited ruling, explain the factual similarities that make the precedent applicable, quote the relevant analytical reasoning, and demonstrate how the same legal principles compel the requested determination in the present case. When potentially adverse rulings exist, distinguish them based on material factual differences, explain why they address different legal questions, or argue that they represent outdated interpretations superseded by subsequent regulatory changes or judicial decisions.

Incorporate relevant judicial precedents from the Court of International Trade and the United States Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit, ensuring proper Bluebook citation format and explaining how judicial interpretations of the tariff schedule, valuation statutes, or rules of origin inform the analysis. When technical or scientific evidence supports the classification or valuation position, reference expert opinions, laboratory analyses, or industry standards, summarizing the methodology and conclusions while noting that complete reports are attached as exhibits.

## Supporting Documentation and Evidence

Compile a comprehensive evidentiary package that substantiates every factual assertion and provides CBP with the information necessary to render an informed determination. Prepare a detailed attachment index that enumerates each exhibit with a brief description of its contents and relevance to the ruling request. Include high-resolution photographs or product samples that visually demonstrate the merchandise's physical characteristics, construction, and distinguishing features, ensuring that images capture relevant details such as material composition, assembly methods, and functional components.

Attach complete technical specifications, engineering drawings, or product schematics that provide precise measurements, material compositions, and manufacturing tolerances. Include manufacturing process descriptions or flow charts that illustrate how raw materials are transformed into the finished product, noting any critical steps that affect classification or origin determination. Provide commercial invoices or pro forma invoices that document the transaction value, payment terms, and any additional charges or adjustments, along with purchase orders or sales contracts that establish the terms of sale and relationship between parties.

Submit any prior CBP rulings, whether binding rulings, internal advice, or protest decisions, that have addressed the same merchandise or substantially similar products, along with any CBP correspondence, CF 28 requests for information, or CF 29 notices of action that provide relevant background. Include expert opinions, laboratory test reports, or technical analyses that support classification or composition claims, ensuring that each expert's qualifications and methodology are clearly documented. When claiming eligibility for preferential trade programs or special tariff treatment, provide all required certifications, declarations of origin, or supporting documentation specified by the applicable trade agreement or statutory provision.

## Certification, Confidentiality, and Procedural Compliance

Conclude with a formal certification statement executed by an individual with personal knowledge of the facts or a reasonable basis for the representations made, affirming under penalty of perjury that all information provided is true, correct, and complete to the best of the signatory's knowledge and belief. The certification must include the printed name, title, and signature of the authorized representative, along with the date of execution. Ensure the signatory possesses actual authority to bind the requesting entity and has reviewed all factual assertions for accuracy.

Address confidentiality considerations by identifying any business proprietary information, trade secrets, or commercially sensitive data included in the ruling request, and explicitly request confidential treatment under 19 CFR 177.2(b)(7). Explain the basis for confidentiality, describing how disclosure would cause substantial competitive harm, and request that CBP redact specified information from any published version of the ruling while maintaining the confidential complete version in CBP's administrative record.

Verify compliance with all procedural requirements under 19 CFR Part 177, confirming that the request is directed to the appropriate CBP office based on the nature of the determination sought, whether the National Commodity Specialist Division for classification matters or the appropriate Center of Excellence and Expertise for industry-specific questions. Ensure the request does not seek determinations on matters excluded from the binding ruling program, such as questions of reasonable care or compliance that are properly addressed through other administrative mechanisms, issues that are the subject of pending litigation or protests, or hypothetical scenarios without concrete importation plans.

## Final Document Assembly and Quality Review

Assemble the complete Binding Ruling Request as a professionally formatted legal document with clear section headings, consistent formatting, and logical organization that guides the reader through the factual background, legal questions, analytical framework, and supporting evidence. Maintain a formal, respectful tone throughout that demonstrates professionalism and regard for CBP's administrative processes while advocating persuasively for the requested determination. Ensure every legal citation is complete and accurate, every factual assertion is supported by documentary evidence, and every analytical conclusion flows logically from the application of law to facts.

Review the completed document for internal consistency, verifying that the merchandise description aligns with attached specifications, the factual narrative supports the legal analysis, and the questions presented correspond to the determinations sought. Confirm that all cross-references to exhibits are accurate, all regulatory citations are current, and all CBP rulings cited remain valid and have not been modified, revoked, or superseded. The final document should reflect thorough preparation, command of customs law, and strategic presentation that maximizes the likelihood of obtaining a favorable binding ruling that provides the certainty necessary for compliant international trade operations.
